summary: Test suite covers all main requirements: version parsing/writing, commit parsing, bump determination, changelog generation, and end-to-end scenarios via act integration tests. However, rigor is limited by sparse edge case testing (e.g., malformed JSON variations, boundary version numbers, scope handling in commits) and missing CLI argument parsing tests. Design is clean with good test isolation via tempfile/separate repos, but hardcoded fixture paths (PROJECT_FILES, ACT_CASES) create brittleness. Integration tests provide real-world validation through act, but the suite would miss CLI regressions, subtle commit-parsing issues, and corner cases in the version logic.
